title: SSH Management
noConnectionsDesc: Create your first SSH connection to get started
selectConnectionDesc: Choose a connection from the list to view details or connect
confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this connection?
confirmDeleteGroup: Are you sure you want to delete this group? Connections will be moved to ungrouped.
passwordRequired: Password is required for password authentication
privateKeyRequired: Private key file is required for private key authentication

form.name: Connection Name
form.saveBeforeInstallKey: Please save the connection before installing the public key
form.defaultDirectoryHint: Must use absolute path, ~ is not supported

migration.selectionHint: Select connections to export a subset
migration.noSelection: Select at least one connection first
migration.exportSensitiveDesc: The exported JSON will contain passwords, passphrases, and private key paths in plain text. Save it only to a trusted location.
migration.importEmptyTitle: Choose a source and parse a file
migration.importEmptyDesc: Flow: choose file → preview → resolve conflicts → import.
migration.knownHostsDetected: known_hosts entries were detected. They are shown as hints only and will not be written into the current config.
migration.privateKeyReviewHint: Private key paths are kept as-is. Check whether they still exist on the new machine.
migration.importFooterHint: Only the SSH section will be updated. Other app settings stay untouched.
migration.importSuccessSummary: Import finished: added {{imported}}, replaced {{replaced}}, duplicated {{duplicated}}, skipped {{skipped}}

dashboard.noSelection: Select a server to view details
dashboard.noSelectionDesc: Click on a connection card or table row to see its details here

workspace.searchHosts: Find a host or ssh user@hostname...
workspace.bannerDesc: Keep credentials, jump hosts, and default directories ready for the next session.
workspace.noSearchMatches: No hosts match the current search. Try another hostname or user.
workspace.newHostHint: Create a new SSH host profile.
workspace.addressHint: Use the hostname or public IP that your SSH client should dial.
workspace.generalHint: Name the host, assign a group, and define the default working path.
workspace.credentialsHint: Choose how this host authenticates and what user the shell should log in as.
workspace.hostPreview: SSH host preview will appear here.
workspace.connectingTitle: Are you sure you want to connect?
workspace.connectingBody: OpenCowork is preparing the secure transport, authenticating your host profile, and waiting for the remote shell to become interactive.
workspace.connectingHint: You can leave this screen open or return to the host list while the session finishes dialing.
workspace.connectFailedBody: The SSH client returned an error before the shell opened. You can review the logs or retry the connection.
workspace.connectFailedHint: Check the credentials, jump host, or server reachability and try again.

sftp.title: SFTP remote workbench
sftp.subtitle: Start with one remote host, then add a second pane only when you need cross-host transfer.
sftp.compareSubtitle: Browse two remote hosts side by side and move files with one transfer model.
sftp.sidebarBody: Assign hosts to the active pane, then add a second pane only when you need compare or transfer.
sftp.paneEmpty: Select a host to start browsing remote files.
sftp.connectHint: Connect this pane before loading the remote directory.
sftp.paneMeta: Pick a host and connect to start.
sftp.selectionEmpty: Select files or folders in either pane to inspect or move them.
sftp.singleSelectionEmpty: Select files or folders in the current pane to inspect or move them.

uploads.title: Uploads
uploads.description: Track compression, upload, remote unzip, and cleanup progress.

terminalTheme.title: Terminal palette
terminalTheme.sheetDescription: Adjust only the SSH terminal and connection titlebar colors without changing the app-wide interface palette.
terminalTheme.description: Pick the SSH terminal background, ANSI palette, selection highlight, and status panel treatment.
terminalTheme.globalHint: Light/dark mode and the interface palette stay in Settings; SSH terminals can use a separate matched palette from the same set.

settings.title: SSH Appearance
settings.description: Switch the SSH host-library interface palette and matched terminal palette.

keychain.labelRequired: Please fill in the key label.
keychain.publicKeyRequired: Please provide the public key content before exporting.
keychain.exportTargetRequired: Please select an SSH host to install the public key.
keychain.exported: Public key installed on target host.
keychain.label: Label
keychain.subtitle: Manage local ~/.ssh keys, public keys, and certificates in one place.
keychain.emptyBody: Create a new key entry, or place existing keys in ~/.ssh.

knownHosts.emptyBody: Trusted host fingerprints will appear here after connecting or adding manual entries.
knownHosts.subtitle: Maintain the SSH host key trust list.

forwarding.required: Please enter a name and select a host.
forwarding.emptyBody: Set up local, remote, and SOCKS forwarding templates here for future reuse.
forwarding.name: Label

snippets.required: Please fill in name and command.
snippets.emptyBody: Save frequently used restart, deploy, and troubleshooting commands for quick reuse.
snippets.subtitle: Store commonly used remote command snippets and ops scripts.
snippets.name: Label

logs.subtitle: Review recent connections, session statuses, and upload activity.

sftp.title: SFTP Workspace
sftp.subtitle: Keep file browsing, upload, and remote directory management outside the terminal.
sftp.emptyBody: Select a host and open a remote file session.
sftp.connectPrompt: You can browse remote directories after the session connects.
sftp.connectHint: The SFTP page reuses or creates an SSH session and keeps file-tree management outside terminal output.
